questions.question: Match the following: Column –I (Organization) Column- II (Founder ) A) British Indian Association 1) JyotibaPhule B) Satyashodhak Samaj 2) Shishir Kumar Ghosh C) ShardaSadan 3) Ramabai D) Indian League 4) Radhakant Deb
questions.explanation: A) British Indian Association – Radhakant Deb (1851) This association was formed in 1851 in Calcutta (now Kolkata) by leaders like Radhakant Deb and Debendranath Tagore. It was one of the earliest political organizations in India. Its aim was to represent the interests of Indians before the British government and demand administrative reforms. B) Satyashodhak Samaj – Jyotiba Phule (1873) The Satyashodhak Samaj was founded in 1873 by Jyotirao Phule in Maharashtra. Its main aim was to promote social equality and fight against caste discrimination and Brahmanical dominance. It worked for the upliftment of lower castes and women. C) Sharda Sadan – Pandita Ramabai (1889) Pandita Ramabai established Sharda Sadan in 1889 in Bombay (Mumbai). It was a home and educational institution for widows and destitute women. It played a significant role in promoting women's education and social reform. D) Indian League – Shishir Kumar Ghosh (1875) The Indian League was founded in 1875 by Shishir Kumar Ghosh. It aimed to develop a sense of nationalism among Indians and encourage political awareness and unity

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true about The Open Champions (Golf) 2025? • The Championship was held at Royal Portrush Golf Club in Northern Ireland from 17-20 July 2025. • The Championship was won by Scottie Scheffier. • The Championship was organized by the PGA Tour.
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is correct: The 2025 Open Championship was scheduled at Royal Portrush Golf Club, Northern Ireland, from 17–20 July 2025. • Statement 2 is correct: Scottie Scheffler won the 2025 Open Championship. • Statement 3 is incorrect: The Open Championship is organized by The R&A, not by the PGA Tour.

questions.question: The sixth phase of “Jal Shakti Abhiyan : Catch the Rain 2025” was launched in which of the following state?
questions.explanation: The 6th phase of Jal Shakti Abhiyan: Catch the Rain 2025 was launched in Haryana. The campaign focuses on water conservation, rainwater harvesting, and sustainable water management, especially before the monsoon season.

questions.question: Which of the following factors does not increase the rate of evaporation?
questions.explanation: Evaporation increases with higher temperature, larger surface area, and higher wind speed. However, when humidity increases, the air already contains more water vapor, so evaporation slows down instead of increasing.

questions.question: Read the following statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R) and choose the correct option. Assertion (A) : Generative design software is increasingly used by ISRO for optimizing structure and weight distribution in aerospace components. Reason (R) : Generative design tools are specially developed for modeling cryogenic fuel tank used in PSLV variants.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true: ISRO and other aerospace agencies use generative design software to optimize structures, reduce weight, and improve efficiency of components. Reason (R) is false: Generative design tools are general-purpose optimization tools and are not specifically developed only for modeling cryogenic fuel tanks or PSLV variants.

questions.question: Dorjilung Hydropower Project is being built between which countries?
questions.explanation: The Dorjilung Hydropower Project is a joint initiative between India and Bhutan. It is being developed in Bhutan with financial and technical assistance from India, and most of the generated electricity is exported to India.

questions.question: Which of the following article of Indian constitution is related to the oath or affirmation by the Governor of Indian State?
questions.explanation: Article 155 – Appointment of Governor This article deals with the appointment of the Governor of a state. The Governor is appointed by the President of India. Article 156 – Term of office of Governor This article explains the tenure of the Governor. The Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President and normally has a term of 5 years. Article 159 – Oath or affirmation by the Governor This article specifically deals with the oath or affirmation taken by the Governor before entering office. Article 158 – Conditions of Governor’s office This article describes the conditions of the Governor’s office, such as not holding any other office of profit and entitlements like salary and allowances.

questions.question: Match the following: Column- I [Organization] Column-II [Year of establishment] A) Asian Development Bank 1) 1961 B) Shangai Corporation 2) 1966 C) European Union 3) 2001 D) Non-Aligned Movement (NAM) 4) 1993
questions.explanation: The correct matching is : A –2, B – 3, C – 4, D – 1 A) Asian Development Bank – 1966 ADB was established in 1966 to promote economic development and cooperation in Asia-Pacific countries. B) Shanghai Cooperation Organization – 2001 SCO was formed in 2001 to promote regional security, economic cooperation, and political stability among member countries. C) European Union – 1993 The EU was officially established in 1993 through the Maastricht Treaty to ensure economic and political integration in Europe. D) Non-Aligned Movement – 1961 NAM was founded in 1961 to represent countries that did not align with any major power bloc during the Cold War.

questions.question: Which of the following places is not associated with the Ashta mahasthana of Gautama Buddha?
questions.explanation: The Ashta Mahasthana are the eight sacred places associated with important events in the life of Gautama Buddha. These include: • Lumbini (birth) • Bodh Gaya (enlightenment) • Sarnath (first sermon) • Kushinagar (Mahaparinirvana) • Rajgir (important teachings) • Vaishali (last sermon / events) • Sankassa (descent from heaven) • Shravasti (miracles) Sanchi is famous for Buddhist stupas and monuments built later, but it is not directly associated with the life events of Buddha, so it is not part of the Ashta Mahasthana.

questions.question: In which of the following Olympics Games did India not win any medal?
questions.explanation: India failed to win a single medal in the 1992 Barcelona Olympics. After winning gold in hockey at the 1980 Moscow Olympics, India went through a long medal drought, returning empty-handed from three consecutive Summer Olympics: 1984 Los Angeles, 1988 Seoul, and 1992 Barcelona. • 1972 Munich : The Indian men's hockey team won a bronze medal after defeating the Netherlands. • 1980 Moscow : India won a gold medal in men's field hockey, which was their 8th and most recent gold in the sport. • 1996 Atlanta : Tennis player Leander Paes won a bronze medal in the men's singles event, ending India's 16-year medal drought.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the RAJA Festival: • It is celebrated mainly in Odisha by women and girls. • The festival is associated with the worship of Mother Earth. • It is a five day festival during the winter season. Which of the above statements is/are true?
questions.explanation: • Statement 1 is true: The RAJA Festival (also known as Raja Parba) is primarily celebrated in the Indian state of Odisha. It is a celebration of womanhood where women and girls are central to the festivities, wearing new clothes, enjoying swings, and being exempted from household chores. • Statement 2 is true: The festival is deeply associated with the worship of Mother Earth (Bhudevi). It is believed that during these days, the Earth undergoes her menstrual cycle and prepares for the upcoming agricultural season. • Statement 3 is incorrect because: • The festival is traditionally three days long, although it may include a fourth day. • The festival occurs in mid-June during the monsoon season, not the winter season.

questions.question: Which of the following carbon compounds burns with a blue, non-sooty flame under normal conditions?
questions.explanation: Ethanol is a clean fuel and burns completely in the presence of sufficient oxygen, producing a blue, non-sooty flame. Diesel and kerosene are hydrocarbons that often burn with a yellow, smoky (sooty) flame due to incomplete combustion. Charcoal generally glows red and does not produce a clear blue flame.

questions.question: Read the following statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R) and choose the correct option. Assertion (A) : Transfer payments are excluded from the calculation of national income. Reason (R) : Transfer payments do not involve any current production of goods or services.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true because transfer payments like pensions, scholarships, subsidies, etc., are not included in national income. Reason (R) is also true because these payments do not correspond to any current production of goods or services. Since national income includes only the value of current production, transfer payments are excluded. Hence, R correctly explains A.

questions.question: Which country hosted the multinational Exercise Sea Dragon-2025 from March 4 to 19, 2025?
questions.explanation: Exercise Sea Dragon-2025 is a multinational anti-submarine warfare exercise hosted by the United States Navy. It took place from March 4 to 19, 2025, and involved participation from countries like India, Japan, Australia, and South Korea.

questions.question: Which five year plan led to the establishment of the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development?
questions.explanation: The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) was established in 1982 on the recommendations of the CRAFICARD Committee. Its establishment is associated with the Sixth Five-Year Plan (1980–85), which focused on agricultural and rural development.

questions.question: In which of the following cases did the Supreme Court hold that a balance between Fundamental Rights and directive Principles of State policy is part of the Basic Structure of the Constitution ?
options: S.R. Bommai Vs Union of India
Minerva Mills Vs Union of India
questions.explanation: In the Minerva Mills vs Union of India (1980) case, the Supreme Court held that harmony and balance between Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles of State Policy is an essential feature of the Basic Structure of the Constitution. • The Court struck down certain provisions of the 42nd Amendment that gave unlimited power to Parliament. • It emphasized that giving absolute primacy to Directive Principles over Fundamental Rights would damage the Constitution’s basic structure. • Thus, maintaining a balance between the two is necessary. Other options: Kesavananda Bharati case (1973): Introduced the Basic Structure doctrine. Golaknath case (1967): Held that Parliament cannot amend Fundamental Rights (later modified). S.R. Bommai case (1994): Related to President’s Rule and federalism.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true about Gupta period? • Vishti (Vaishthika) referred to forced labour imposed by the state. • Devadana referred to tax-free land, grants specifically given to Brahmins. • Pustapala related to the keeper of records and notary during Gupta Period.
questions.explanation: Statement 2 is incorrect: Devadana refers to land grants given to temples or deities, not specifically to Brahmins (those were called Brahmadeya). Statements 1 and 3 are correct

questions.question: Where should an object be placed in front of a convex lens to get a real image of the size of the object?
options: At the Principal focus of the lens
Between infinity and the principal focus of the lens
questions.explanation: For a convex lens: • When the object is placed at 2F (twice the focal length), the image is formed at 2F on the other side. • The image is real, inverted, and of the same size as the object. Other cases: • At infinity → image at focus, highly diminished • At focus → image at infinity • Between infinity and focus → image size varies (generally diminished)

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true? • Ragi crop is associated with dryland farming. • Wheat is an example of temperate and subtropical crop.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Ragi (finger millet) is a hardy crop that grows well in low rainfall areas and is commonly associated with dryland farming. • Statement 2 is correct: Wheat is primarily a temperate crop, but it is also widely grown in subtropical regions like India during the winter season (Rabi crop). Hence, both statements are correct.

questions.question: Which of the following statements with reference to the preamble to the Constitution of India are correct? • The word ‘Secular’ comes before the word ‘Socialist’ • The word ‘Justice’ comes before the word ‘Equality’ • The word ‘Fraternity’ comes after the word ‘Liberty’
questions.explanation: The correct statements are 2 and 3 only. Correct order in the Preamble of the Constitution of India: “Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic…” “Justice, Liberty, Equality and Fraternity…”

questions.question: Which traditional dance of Mizoram is performed to celebrate victory in war?
questions.explanation: So Lakia is a traditional war dance of Mizoram, performed to celebrate victory in battles. It reflects bravery and the warrior culture of the Mizo people. Other options: Kodava – Associated with Karnataka Chamba – A region in Himachal Pradesh Dumhal – A dance form of Jammu & Kashmir

questions.question: Consider the following statements: • Sanchi Stupa was rediscovered in 1814 by General Taylor and the inscriptions were successfully deciphered for the first time in 1840 by James Prinsep. • It was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1989 • The Chhatra at Sanchi consists of three superimposed umbrellas symbolizing the three Jewels of Buddhism. Which of the following statements is/are true?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect:- The Sanchi Stupa was rediscovered in 1818 (not 1814) by General Henry Taylor. Also, the inscriptions were deciphered by James Prinsep in 1837, not 1840. Statement 2 is correct:- Sanchi Stupa was declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1989. Statement 3 is correct:-The Chhatra (umbrella) at the top of the stupa has three tiers representing the Three Jewels of Buddhism — Buddha, Dhamma, and Sangha.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents Mendel’s conclusion about inheritance?
options: Each trait is controlled by three genes
Each traits is controlled by one factor
Each trait is controlled by two factors
Each trait is controlled by one chromosome.
questions.explanation: According to Gregor Mendel, each trait is controlled by a pair of factors (now called genes). One factor is inherited from each parent, which together determine the expression of a trait.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are true? • Svapnavasavadattam was composed by Bhasa. • The play is based on Buddhist ideals of compassion and self-sacrifice.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct:- Svapnavasavadattam is a famous Sanskrit play written by Bhasa, one of the earliest known Indian dramatists. Statement 2 is incorrect:- The play is not based on Buddhist ideals. It is a romantic and political drama centered around King Udayana and Queen Vasavadatta. It draws from courtly life and royal themes, not Buddhist philosophy.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a tributary river of Krishna River?
questions.explanation: Panchganga, Ghatprabha, and Koyna are tributaries of the Krishna River. Indravati is not a tributary of the Krishna River; it is a major tributary of the Godavari River.

questions.question: In a municipal committee election, three candidates X,Y and Z are competing. Out of a total registered population of 12,000, only 80% of the people cast their votes. There were no invalid or NOTA votes recorded. candidate Z received the fewest votes, totaling exactly 480 votes and candidate X won the election by securing 55% of the total votes cast. How many votes did Y received?
questions.explanation: Total votes which were cast =12000×80/100=9600 X got 55% of the cast votes, i.e. =55/100×9600=5280 Z got exactly 480 votes and there were no invalid votes recorded. Thus, Y share of votes =9600-(5280+480) = 3840

questions.question: Three solid metal cubes with edges of 6cm, 8cm and 10 cm are melted together to form a single large cube. During the melting process, it is found that 397 cm² of the metal is lost due to improper handling. What is total surface area of newly formed cubs?
questions.explanation: Total volume of three cubes before improper handling = 6³ + 8³ + 10³ = 216 + 512 + 1000 = 1728 cm³ Actual volume of the large cube after 397 cm³ metal is lost due to improper handling = 1728 cm³ - 397 cm³ = 1331 cm³. So, each side of the new cube = ∛1331 = 11 cm Hence, Total surface area = 6 × 11² = 6 × 121 = 726 cm²

questions.question: If secA+tanA=3 then find the value of sinA.
questions.explanation: It is given that, sec A + tan A = 3 ……(i) ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_c75afb5a.png) As we know, sec² A - tan² A = 1 Or, (sec A + tan A)(sec A - tan A) = 1 So, sec A - tan A = ⅓ ……(ii) By adding equation (i) and (ii) – 2 sec A = 3 + ⅓ = 10/3 ⇒ sec A = 5/3, so, sin A = ⅘

questions.question: Find the fourth proportion of 45, 75 and 120.
questions.explanation: Let’s say the fourth proportion is x. Thus, 45/75=120/x ⇒x =(120×75)/45=200. Hence, the fourth proportion is 200.

questions.question: An amount of money doubles itself in 8 years at a certain rate of simple interest per annum. What is the rate of interest per annum?
questions.explanation: Let the principal amount be P and the rate of interest be r % p.a. Then amount P becomes 2P at r % p.a. in 8 years. So, interest during this period is (2P-P)=P. So, P=(P×8×r)/100⇒r=100/8=12.5%

questions.question: Four numbers are in the ratio of 2:3:5:7, if the difference between second and fourth number is 94. find the sum of second and third number.
questions.explanation: It is given, ratio of four numbers = 2: 3: 5: 7 Difference between 2ⁿᵈ and 4ᵗʰ number = (7-3) = 4 units →94 Thus, 1 unit →23.5 2ⁿᵈ number + 3ʳᵈ number = (3+5) units=8 units=8×23.5=188

questions.question: Ravi travels from home to his workplace at a speed of ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_33f59a8d.gif) and returns home along the same route at a speed of ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_7e536f3d.gif) . If the entire round trip takes a total of 5 hours. What is the distance between his home and the workplace?
questions.explanation: If distance is constant then speed is inversely proportional to time. Here, Speed ratio 12 : 3 = 4 : 1 So, Time ratio 3 : 12 = 1 : 4 As the entire round trip takes him a total of 5 hours, we can say that when he travels with ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_33f59a8d.gif) , he takes 1 hour and when he travels with 3 km/hr, he takes 4 hours. So, the distance between his home and workplace is = 12 × 1 km = 3 × 4 km = 12 km.

questions.question: A regular polygon has three times the number of sides as another regular polygon. The perimeter of the smaller polygon is 60 cm and each of its interior angle measures 120°. The side length of the larger polygon is 0.5 times that of the smaller one. What is the measure of each interior angle of the larger polygon?
questions.explanation: Given, Interior angle of the smaller polygon is 120°. If the number of the sides of a polygon is n, its interior angle is (n−2)×180⁰/n=120⁰ ⇒ 180n - 360 = 120n ⇒ n = 6 (Hexagon) Thus, the number of the sides of the larger polygon is 6 × 3 = 18. So, each interior angle of the larger polygon is (n−2)180⁰/ n =16×180⁰/18 =160⁰

questions.question: The coordinates of the vertices of a triangle are A(0,0)B(3,0) and C(0,4). What is the location of orthocenter?
questions.explanation: It is evident from the given coordinates of three vertices of the triangle that it is a right-angled triangle with right angle at A(0,0), and two legs of right angle are 3 units and 4 units. In any right-angled tringle, the orthocenter always lies exactly on the vertex containing the right angle. So, the location of the orthocenter of the triangle is (0,0).

questions.question: Which of the following term describes a straight line that intersects a circle at exactly two distinct points?
questions.explanation: Tangent - Touches a circle at one point. Secant -Intersects a circle at exactly two points. Chord - A line segment connecting two distinct points on a circle. Arc - A segment of circumference of a circle, it is an open curved line not a straight line. Hence, a straight line that intersects a circle at exactly two distinct points is called Secant.

questions.question: A Shopkeeper purchases 120 notebooks for ₹4800. He sells 80 of them at ₹50 and the remaining notebooks at ₹35 each. What is his overall profit percentage?
A retailer offers two successive discounts of 15% and 10% on a digital camera. What is the single equivalant discount percentage?
questions.explanation: Equivalent discount percentage=(-15-10+(15×10)/100)% =(-25 + 1.5 ) %= - 23.5 %

questions.question: The base of a light prism is a right-angled triangle with hypotenuse of 13 cm and one leg of 5 cm. If the height of the prism is 15 cm. find the lateral surface area of the prism.
questions.explanation: Since the base is a right-angled triangle with one side 5 cm and hypotenuse 13 cm, another side will be √(13²-5²)=12 cm. So, Perimeter of the base is = (5+12+13) cm=30 cm. Height of the prism is 15 cm given. So, Lateral Surface area of a prism = Perimeter of base × height of the prism =30 cm ×15 cm=450 cm²

questions.question: The base of a triangle is 12 cm and its height is 15 cm. If the area of this triangle is equal to the area of a rectangle with breadth of 9 cm. What is the length of rectangle?
questions.explanation: Let the length of the rectangle be l cm. Now, Area of Δ= Area of rectangle. ½×12×15=l×9 ⇒ l=10 cm

questions.question: The mean of the data set 6,11,8,14,2,13 is k. and another data set consisting of 15,22,11,45,8,-32, x has a mean of k + 4 and a median of y. Find the value of (x+y).
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_8f4e4a7c.png)

questions.question: If √x = 23 then what is the value of x - √x ?

The average marks of a class of 25 students was calculated as 64. Later it was discovered that the marks of one student were wrongly entered as 42 instead of 92. What is the correct average marks of the class?
questions.explanation: Change in the total sum of the marks of the class due to wrongful entry of one student’s marks = wrong entry - correct entry = 42-92=-50. So, after the correction, total sum of the marks will be increased by +50. Average marks of the class will be increased by = (+50)/25=+2. Hence, correct average marks of the class = 64+2=66.

questions.question: The value of √(7+2√10) is:
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_d305f265.png)

questions.question: Three partners A, B and C start a business. B 's capital is four times C 's capital, and one-third of A 's capital is equal to B's capital. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹51,000. Find A 's share of the profit.
questions.explanation: Let C's capital be x. Then, B's capital = 4x. and, ⅓ A's capital = B's capital. ⇒ A = 3B = 12x. so, A:B:C = 12:4:1 Now, let A, B and C's shares of profit be 12y, 4y and y, respectively. Total profit, 17y → ₹51,000 ⇒ y = ₹3,000 A's share of profit = 12 × ₹3000 = ₹36,000

questions.question: Three types of pulses costing ₹80 per kg, ₹95 per kg and a third variety are mixed in the ratio 2:1:2. If the average cost of the resulting mixture is ₹115 per kg, what is the price of the third variety per kg?
questions.explanation: Let the price of the third variety of pulse be ₹x per kg. Average price of all three varieties of pulses =₹115. Ratio of these three varieties of pulses given = 2:1:2 By weighted average method– (80×2+95×1+x×2)/(2+1+2)=115 ⇒160+95+2x=575 ⇒ 2x=320 ⇒ x=160 Hence, the price of the third variety is ₹160 per kg.

questions.question: A painter's ladder of length 25 m is leaning against a vertical wall. It makes an angle θ with the ground such that sinθ=24/25. Find the horizontal distance from the base of the wall to the foot of the ladder.
questions.explanation: In △ABC- ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848651Math%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_bd557bea.png) sin θ = AB/AC ⇒ 24/25 = AB/25 So, AB = 24. Again, In △ABC- AB² + BC² = AC² ⇒ (24)² + BC² = (25)² ⇒ BC = 7 m Hence, the horizontal distance from the base of the wall to the foot of the ladder is 7 m.

questions.question: 24 workers can complete a construction project in 30 days. After working for 15 days, 9 workers are pulled off the site to work on another project. In how many days will the remaining workers finish the task?
questions.explanation: Total work =24×30=720 unit. After work of 15 days out of 30 days done by 24 workers, remaining work =24×(30-15)=360 unit. After 9 workers are pulled off, number of remaining workers =24-9=15 So, Days required to finish the remaining work by 15 workers = 360/15=24 days.

questions.question: Rahul took a loan of ₹ 20,000 at 12% compound interest per annum for 3 years. He repaid ₹ 10,000 at the end of 1st year and remaining balance at the end of 3ʳᵈ year. Find the total interest paid by Rahul.
questions.explanation: Amount after end of the 1st year at 12% compound interest = ₹20,000 × (1+12/100) = ₹22,400. After repayment of ₹10,000 at the end of the 1st year, principal amount for the next 2 years becomes– ₹22,400 - ₹10,000 = ₹12,400 Amount at the end of 3rd year– ⇒ ₹12,400 (1+12/100)² = ₹12400 × 28/25 × 28/25 = ₹15554.56 Total Interest paid by Rahul: (₹10,000 + ₹15554.56) - ₹20,000 = ₹5554.56

questions.question: A bag contains 5 red,4 green and 3 blue balls. If three balls are drawn at random, what is the probability that at least two of them are red?
questions.explanation: Reqⁿ probability = (Favourable outcome)/(Total outcome) There are two possible scenarios of favourable outcomes: (2 red balls + 1 ball of other two colors) and (all 3 red balls). Total number of favourable outcomes for 2 red balls + 1 other ball is ⁵C₂ × ⁷C₁ = 70. Total number of favourable outcomes for all 3 red balls is ⁵C₃ × ⁷C₀ = 10. So, total number of favourable outcomes is 70+10=80. Total number of outcomes of drawing 3 balls out of 12 balls is ¹²C₃ =220 Hence, the Probability of drawing at least 2 red balls is 80/220=4/11

questions.question: Which of the following number is divisible by 8?
questions.explanation: According to divisibility rule of 8, a number is divisible by 8 if its last three digits is divisible by 8. Clearly, after checking all the four options according to above mentioned condition, 8248 (last three digits is 248) is divisible by 8.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation: Passersby is the correct plural form of "passerby." In compound nouns formed with a noun and a preposition/adverb, the "s" is added to the principal word (the noun), not the end of the phrase. Correct Sentence: The local police interviewed several Passersby who witnessed the accident.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. "The young heir loved to __________ his expensive collection of vintage cars whenever guests visited the estate."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- show off. 📌 Explanation: • Show off (दिखावा करना / शेखी बघारना): To display something proudly or ostentatiously. • Show up: To arrive or appear at a place, often late or unexpectedly. • Show down: This is usually a noun (showdown), meaning a final argument or test to settle a dispute. • Show out: This isn't a standard phrasal verb used in this context; you might "show someone out" (lead them to the exit), but you don't "show out" a car collection.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. If the architect would have noticed the flaw in the foundation earlier, the entire structure would not have collapsed during the storm.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- had noticed. 📌 Explanation — The "If + Had" Rule: When we talk about an imaginary situation in the past (something that didn't actually happen), the sentence must follow this strict structure: • The "If" Clause: Must use Had + V3 (Past Perfect). • The Main Clause: Must use Would have + V3. Correct Sentence: If the architect had noticed the flaw in the foundation earlier, the entire structure would not have collapsed during the storm.

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: Neither the size nor the colours is right.
The poet and dramatist is dead today itself.
Everyone of the two girls has done well.
Gold as well as silver are precious metals.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- The poet and dramatist is dead today itself. 📌 Explanation - This sentence is grammatically correct because it follows the rule of Single Identity. When two nouns are joined by "and" but preceded by a single article (The), they refer to the same person. Since the poet and the dramatist are one individual, the singular verb "is" is correct. Explanation for other wrong options: • Option A: Error in the "Rule of Proximity" with "Neither... nor". When subjects are joined by "nor," the verb must agree with the nearest subject. Since the nearest subject "colours" is plural, the verb must also be plural. So, replace the singular "is" with the plural "are." • Option C: Error in Distributive Pronouns with "Everyone". The word "Everyone" is used for more than two people. When referring specifically to two people or things, we must use "Each" or "Either." So, replace "Everyone" with "Each." • Option D: Error in Subject-Verb Agreement with "as well as". When two subjects are joined by phrases like "as well as," "along with," or "together with," the verb must agree with the first subject. Since "Gold" is singular, the verb must also be singular. So, replace "are" with "is."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- Omission. 📌 Explanation – Omission (चूक) refers to someone or something that has been left out or excluded, or the failure to do something that one is morally or legally bound to do.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. In a sea of average candidates, her attention to detail and her placid demeanour made her stand up as the most qualified applicant for the senior position.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- stand out. 📌 Explanation — Stand out (अलग दिखना / विशिष्ट होना): To be very easy to see or notice because of being different or better. In this context, her "meticulous" and "placid" qualities made her better than the "average candidates."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - Unlike birds that lay eggs, most mammals are viviparous, giving birth to developed offspring.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- Live-bearing. 📌 Explanation - Viviparous is a biological term referring to animals that bring forth live young that have developed inside the body of the parent. • Live-bearing: जीवित बच्चों को जन्म देने वाला - Producing live young instead of eggs. • Oviparous: अंडज - Producing young by means of eggs that are hatched after they have been laid by the parent. • Inanimate: निर्जीव - Not alive, especially not in the manner of animals and humans. • Dormant: सुप्त - Having normal physical functions suspended or slowed down for a period; in a deep sleep.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Phony
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- Genuine. 📌 Explanation – • Phony refers to something or someone that is not genuine; fake or fraudulent. • Genuine: असली / वास्तविक - Truly what something is said to be; authentic. • Bogus: जाली - Not genuine or true; fake. • Counterfeit: जाली मुद्रा - Made in exact imitation of something valuable with the intention to deceive.

questions.question: Identify the Sentence Type. "The diligent student studied throughout the night, but the results of the mock exam were only ephemeral."
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- Compound Sentence. 📌 Explanation — A Compound Sentence is like a marriage of two equal parts. It contains two independent clauses (sentences that can stand alone) joined by a coordinating conjunction. • Clause 1: "The diligent student studied throughout the night" (Complete thought). • The Connector: "but" • Clause 2: "the results of the mock exam were only ephemeral" (Complete thought).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. The manager blamed the employee for the argument, but everyone knew that it takes two to tango.
options: One person is entirely responsible for the situation
Both parties involved are responsible for the outcome
It is easy to solve a problem with teamwork
A conflict can only be settled by a third party
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation - It takes two to tango (एक हाथ से ताली नहीं बजती / दोनों पक्ष जिम्मेदार होना) is used to suggest that if a conflict or a specific situation arises, both people involved are equally responsible for it.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- worked very hardly 📌 Explanation — The "Hard vs. Hardly" Trap: • Hard: This word is both an adjective and an adverb. It means with great effort or strength. • Example: He works hard. • Hardly: This is an adverb, but it means "almost not" or "scarcely." • Example: He hardly works. Correct Sentence: The meticulous student worked very hard to pass the competitive exam.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. Amity
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - Amity refers to a state of friendship and peaceful relations between two people, groups, or countries. • Animosity: बैर / शत्रुता - Strong hostility or ill will. • Discord: कलह - Disagreement between people; lack of harmony. • Hostility: शत्रुता - Unfriendly or opposed behaviour; opposition.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- The host was so loquacious that the guests hardly had a chance to speak during the entire dinner party.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- Reticent. 📌 Explanation – • Loquacious means tending to talk a great deal; talkative. Its antonym must describe someone who is reserved or says very little. • Reticent: अल्पभाषी / मौन रहने वाला - Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily; reserved. • Garrulous: बातूनी - Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. • Voluble: धाराप्रवाह बोलने वाला - Speaking or spoken incessantly and fluently. • Chatty: गप्पी - Fond of or characterized by casual conversation.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. "Hardly ________ station when the train whistled."
options: had he reached the old railway
he had reached at the big
did he reached the busy local
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- had he reached the old railway. 📌 Explanation - It follows the rule of Inversion with Negative Adverbs. When a sentence starts with "Hardly," "Scarcely," or "No sooner," the auxiliary verb (had/did) must come before the subject (he). Additionally, "Hardly" is correctly paired with the conjunction "when." Correct Sentence: "Hardly had he reached the old railway station when the train whistled."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. A person who is out to destroy all established government, law and order
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- Anarchist. 📌 Explanation – • Anarchist is a person who believes in or tries to bring about a state of lawlessness or a society without a central governing body. • Monarchist: राजतंत्रवादी - A person who supports the principle of having a king or queen as the head of state. • Diplomat: राजनयिक - An official representing a country abroad. • Autocrat: तानाशाह - A ruler who has absolute power.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Benjamin’s portion
options: A very small or insignificant amount
A portion of food that is left over
A gift given to a younger brother
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D). 📌 Explanation - Benjamin’s portion refers to the largest share or a much bigger portion of something compared to others. It originates from the biblical story of Joseph giving his youngest brother Benjamin five times as much as his other brothers. Example: When the inheritance was divided, the eldest son received Benjamin's portion of the family estate.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. A person who is recovering from illness
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (D)- Convalescent. 📌 Explanation – • Convalescent refers to a person who is gradually recovering their health and strength after an illness or medical treatment. • Invalid: बीमार / अशक्त - A person made weak or disabled by illness or injury. • Senile: सठियाया हुआ - Showing a loss of mental ability associated with old age. • Agnostic: अज्ञेयवादी - A person who believes that nothing is known or can be known of the existence or nature of God.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P: but the results were only ephemeral Q: because the team failed to maintain R: the initial success of the project brought great joy S: their meticulous approach during the second phase
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- RPQS. 📌 Explanation — • R (The Start): "The initial success... brought great joy" (This introduces the positive beginning of the story). • P (The Contrast): "...but the results were only ephemeral" (The word "but" creates a contrast. "Ephemeral" means short-lived, which directly opposes the "joy" and "success" in R). • Q (The Reason): "...because the team failed to maintain" (This explains why the success was short-lived). • S (The Detail): "...their meticulous approach during the second phase." (This completes the thought in Q—what exactly did they fail to maintain? Their careful approach). Full Correct Sentence: "The initial success of the project brought great joy, but the results were only ephemeral because the team failed to maintain their meticulous approach during the second phase."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. The difficult assignment should have been completed by Panth.
options: Panth should have completed the difficult assignment.
Panth should complete the difficult assignment.
Panth should had completed the difficult assignment.
Panth should have been completing the difficult assignment.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A). 📌 Explanation: • Tense Integrity: The original sentence uses "should have been completed." This is a past obligation. To make it active, we use "should have + V3." • Removing the Passive Marker: In perfect tenses, the word "been" is what makes it passive. In the active voice, "been" must be removed. • Modal Grammar Rules: Option (C) is a major grammatical trap. In English, we never say "should had." Modals are always followed by the base form "have," regardless of the subject or the timing.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. The policeman said to the driver, "Show me your license."
options: The policeman ordered the driver that he should show him his license.
The policeman ordered the driver to show him his license.
The policeman said to the driver to show him my license.
The policeman ordered the driver to show me his license.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B). 📌 Explanation: • The Reporting Verb: Since a policeman has official authority, "ordered" is the most grammatically appropriate reporting verb for a command. • The "To + V1" Rule: In imperative sentences (commands/requests), we do not use "that." Instead, we use the infinitive "to" as a connector. • Pronoun Shift (The Person): The word "me" in direct speech refers to the policeman. In indirect speech, it must change to "him." • Pronoun Shift (The Possession): The word "your" refers to the driver. In indirect speech, it changes to "his."

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Soft Power," coined by Joseph Nye, refers to a nation's ability to influence others through attraction and persuasion rather than the "Hard Power" of military force or economic coercion. In the 21st century, a country’s cultural exports—such as cinema, cuisine, and technology—serve as potent tools of diplomacy. For instance, the global popularity of a nation's film industry can shape international perceptions, making the country appear more "relatable" and trustworthy. However, critics argue that reliance on soft power is insufficient in a "Realist" geopolitical landscape where territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain paramount. They suggest that "Smart Power"—a strategic combination of both hard and soft elements—is the only viable path for a state to maintain its sovereignty and global standing in an increasingly fragmented world. Question 1: What is the defining characteristic of "Soft Power" according to the text?
options: The use of military intervention to settle disputes.
A nation's reliance on economic sanctions.
The ability to influence through attraction and persuasion.
The total abandonment of all cultural exports.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- The ability to influence through attraction and persuasion. 📌 Explanation: The passage defines Soft Power as a nation's ability to influence "through attraction and persuasion rather than... military force."

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Soft Power," coined by Joseph Nye, refers to a nation's ability to influence others through attraction and persuasion rather than the "Hard Power" of military force or economic coercion. In the 21st century, a country’s cultural exports—such as cinema, cuisine, and technology—serve as potent tools of diplomacy. For instance, the global popularity of a nation's film industry can shape international perceptions, making the country appear more "relatable" and trustworthy. However, critics argue that reliance on soft power is insufficient in a "Realist" geopolitical landscape where territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain paramount. They suggest that "Smart Power"—a strategic combination of both hard and soft elements—is the only viable path for a state to maintain its sovereignty and global standing in an increasingly fragmented world. Question 2: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a tool of soft power?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (A)- Military expansionism 📌 Explanation: The passage lists cinema, cuisine, and technology as tools of soft power. Military expansionism is a form of "Hard Power" and is not a tool of soft power.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Soft Power," coined by Joseph Nye, refers to a nation's ability to influence others through attraction and persuasion rather than the "Hard Power" of military force or economic coercion. In the 21st century, a country’s cultural exports—such as cinema, cuisine, and technology—serve as potent tools of diplomacy. For instance, the global popularity of a nation's film industry can shape international perceptions, making the country appear more "relatable" and trustworthy. However, critics argue that reliance on soft power is insufficient in a "Realist" geopolitical landscape where territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain paramount. They suggest that "Smart Power"—a strategic combination of both hard and soft elements—is the only viable path for a state to maintain its sovereignty and global standing in an increasingly fragmented world. Question 3: Why do critics believe "Soft Power" alone is insufficient?
options: Because cinema and cuisine have no impact on diplomacy.
Because territorial and resource-based conflicts are still highly significant.
Because most nations have already adopted Smart Power.
Because soft power is too expensive for developing nations to maintain.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- Because territorial and resource-based conflicts are still highly significant. 📌 Explanation: Critics argue that soft power is insufficient because territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain "paramount" in the real world. Look for words like "However" or "Critics argue." The author uses these to show the limitations of the main concept.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Soft Power," coined by Joseph Nye, refers to a nation's ability to influence others through attraction and persuasion rather than the "Hard Power" of military force or economic coercion. In the 21st century, a country’s cultural exports—such as cinema, cuisine, and technology—serve as potent tools of diplomacy. For instance, the global popularity of a nation's film industry can shape international perceptions, making the country appear more "relatable" and trustworthy. However, critics argue that reliance on soft power is insufficient in a "Realist" geopolitical landscape where territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain paramount. They suggest that "Smart Power"—a strategic combination of both hard and soft elements—is the only viable path for a state to maintain its sovereignty and global standing in an increasingly fragmented world. Question 4: What is the meaning of the word "Paramount" as used in the passage?
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (C)- supreme. 📌 Explanation: In the context of geopolitics, if a dispute is paramount, it means it is of the highest priority or importance. • Coercion(जबरदस्ती/बलप्रयोग): The practice of persuading someone to do something by using force or threats. • Sovereignty(संप्रभुता): The authority of a state to govern itself.

questions.question: Direction: Read the passage and answer the following questions. The concept of "Soft Power," coined by Joseph Nye, refers to a nation's ability to influence others through attraction and persuasion rather than the "Hard Power" of military force or economic coercion. In the 21st century, a country’s cultural exports—such as cinema, cuisine, and technology—serve as potent tools of diplomacy. For instance, the global popularity of a nation's film industry can shape international perceptions, making the country appear more "relatable" and trustworthy. However, critics argue that reliance on soft power is insufficient in a "Realist" geopolitical landscape where territorial disputes and resource scarcity remain paramount. They suggest that "Smart Power"—a strategic combination of both hard and soft elements—is the only viable path for a state to maintain its sovereignty and global standing in an increasingly fragmented world. Question 5: What does the term "Smart Power" imply in the context of the passage?
options: Relying exclusively on advanced AI technology for defense.
A balanced approach using attractive influence and physical force.
Using soft power to hide a country's economic weaknesses.
The process of educating a nation's population to be smarter.
questions.explanation: 👉 The correct answer is option (B)- A balanced approach using both attractive influence and physical force. 📌 Explanation: The passage defines Smart Power as a "strategic combination of both hard and soft elements." To find the meaning of "Smart Power," you have to combine the definitions of "Hard" (force) and "Soft" (attraction) as provided earlier in the text.

questions.question: What day of the week was 17 October 2013?
questions.explanation: date → 17 month code → 0 last two digits of Year → 13 (last two digit of year /4) → 3 Year code → 6 Add all these ⇒ 39 divide 39 by 7 ⇒ 39/7 = 5 (Remainder 4 ⇒ day code) for Thursday

questions.question: Few of the letters are given followed by a question mark (?) these letters are following a Specific pattern you have to give the correct option which can replace question mark. What Comes after: C, H, O, X, I, ?
questions.explanation: Convert letters into positions: C=3, H=8, O=15, X=24, I=9 Now check the differences: 3→8 = +5 8→15 = +7 15→24 = +9 24→9(35) = +11 → I can be written as 9 and 26+9=35 9→+13=22→V

questions.question: Select the option that best represents the correct order of the given words as they would appear in an English dictionary. 1. Market 2. Marble 3. Margin 4. Marine 5. Mark 6. March
questions.explanation: The correct order is • Marble (2) • March (6) • Margin (3) • Marine (4) • Mark (5) • Market (1) ⇒ Final confirmed order: 2,6,3,4,5,1

questions.question: In a coded language, REASON is coded as 9, MARKET is coded as 8 and SCHOOL is coded as 8. What will be the code for GARDEN?
questions.explanation: Code = number of consonants × number of vowels. REASON → 3×3=9 MARKET → 4×2=8 SCHOOL → 4×2=8 ⇒ GARDEN → 4×2=8

questions.question: Each question has four options. Three of them go together based on a common idea or rule. One is different. Identify the one that is different.
questions.explanation: Pattern :- Second number ×7+1= First number Opt B) 6×7+1=43 Opt C) 7×7+1=50 Opt D) 9×7+1=64 Opt A) 5×6+1=31 × hence, option A is different.

questions.question: CF is to DE as KN is to?
questions.explanation: Pattern: C→D (+1) F→E (-1) First letter moves ahead while second moves backward. Similarly, K→L (+1) N→M (-1) ⇒ KN is to LM.

questions.question: If '+' means '-', '-' means '×', '×' means '÷' and '÷' means '+', then find the value of: 20+4-2×6÷3

In the diagram given below, the triangle represents Athletes, the circle represents Swimmers, and the rectangle represents Medalists. The number in each area represents the number of Individuals in that category. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848677Rsng%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_7306afe0.png) Statements: I. The number of medalists who are only Swimmers is 11. II. The total number of Athletes is 8 less than the total number of Swimmers. Which of the following options is correct?
questions.explanation: I. 'Medalists who are only swimmers' refers to the intersection of Rectangle and circle, excluding the triangle. looking at the data the value is 11. Statement I is correct. II. Total Athletes (triangle) =14+9+6+5=34. Total Swimmers (circle) =12+11+9+5=37 ⇒ 37-34=3. The total no. of Athletes is 3 less than Swimmers, not 8 . Statement II is incorrect.

questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following number Series? 5, 6, 2, 11, -5, ?

questions.question: 6 persons P,Q,R,S,T and U are sitting in a row facing north (not necessarily in the Same order). T is to the immediate left of Q,R is the immediate neighbour of P and T. Some persons are to the right of U.S is not at any of the ends of the row. Who is sitting second to the right of U?
questions.explanation: USPRTQ

questions.question: Each question has four options. Three of them go together based on a common rule. One is different. Identify the one that is different.
questions.explanation: Pattern: 1st number × 3 + 5 = 2nd number 9 × 3 + 5 = 32 11 × 3 + 5 = 38 14 × 3 + 5 = 47 16 × 3 + 5 = 53 ≠ 54 Hence, option 4 is the answer.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers is different from the rest? 150, 392, 1452, 2366, 809
In the following question, select the number that will replace the question mark (?) to complete the analogy: 42:156::72:?

Post: Sales executive. Branding department eligibility criteria (as on 01-01-2026): • Must have completed a bachelor's degree in Business administration (BBA) or any related field with at least 50% marks. • Age should be between 23 and 29 years. • Must have a good understanding of brand promotion and Customer behavior. • Strong Presentation Skills are required. Case: Ritika is 25 years old, has completed her BBA with 58% marks, and has excellent presentation skills but does not have experience in brand promotion. What should be done in Ritika's case?
options: Ritika is eligible for the role.
Ritika is not eligible due to lack of brand promotion experience.
Ritika is ineligible because she has not completed her degree in branding.
Ritika is not eligible as she is below the minimum age limit.
questions.explanation: All eligibility conditions must be checked. • Required degree :- BBA or related field with at least 50% marks → Ritika has BBA with ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848677Rsng%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_e72ca2d8.gif) . ✓ • Age limit:- 23-29 years → Ritika is 25 years old. ✓ • Presentation Skills required :- She has excellent Presentation skills. ✓ • Experience in brand promotion is not mentioned as mandatory, only understanding is required. Hence, Ritika satisfies all mandatory criteria.

questions.question: from the given alternatives, select the word that CANNOT be formed by using the letters of the given word. TRANSACTION
questions.explanation: 'F' is not available in TRANSACTION hence, option (3) FACTION cannot be formed.

questions.question: In this question, four words have been given, out of which whose the odd one out.
questions.explanation: PLANT → CCVCC BRICK → CCVCC STORM → CCVCC AUDIO → VVCVV ✓ (different pattern) ⇒ Opt 4 has different pattern than the others. ⇒ C stands for consonant; V stands for vowel.

questions.question: Each item describes a situation and is followed by four possible responses. Indicate the response you find most appropriate. Choose only one response for the item. The responses will be evaluated based on the level of appropriateness for the given situation. Rohan has two training opportunities: one at a well-known corporate company where he will mostly handle documentation work, and another at a small tech firm where he will learn software testing and basic development. He wants to build a career in the IT field. Which should he choose?
options: Join the corporate company for brand value.
Join the tech firm for practical skill development.
Try to manage both trainings simultaneously.
Reject both and prepare only through online videos.
questions.explanation: Rohan wants to build a career in the IT field, so the option that helps him gain relevant technical skills and practical experience is most appropriate. Option B directly supports his IT career. Therefore, it is the most appropriate answer.

questions.question: By interchanging the given two numbers (not digits), which of the following equations will NOT be correct? 3 and 5 I. 7×5+3-14÷7=36 II. 9×3+7-21÷7=47
questions.explanation: I. ⇒ 7×3 + 5 - 14÷7 21 + 5 - 2 = 24 ≠ 36 I is not correct. II. ⇒ 9×5 + 7 - 21÷7 45 + 7 - 3 = 49 ≠ 47 II is not correct. Therefore, both I and II are not correct.

questions.question: In the question below there are three statements followed by two conclusions I and II. You have to take the three given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the three statements disregarding commonly proven facts. Statements: All Truck are Hilux. Some Hilux are Ford. Only Ford are Flat. Conclusions: • Some Truck are not Flat. • All Ford can never be Hilux.

questions.question: 5 persons P,Q,R,S and T are sitting around a circular table facing towards the centre (not necessarily in the same order). Q is sitting second to the right of S. P is sitting second to the right of Q.R is not an immediate neighbour of P. Who is sitting third to the right of R ?
questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848677Rsng%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_b48e6146.png)

questions.question: 'Train tickets fares will be reduced by ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848677Rsng%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_d67e61cd.gif) for senior citizens." Which assumption is implicit?
options: All trains run with empty seats.
Lower fares will encourage more senior citizens to travel by train.
Senior citizens are discouraged from traveling currently.
Senior citizens can easily afford the present ticket fares.
questions.explanation: The statement reduces train fares for senior citizens, which implies the decision is made to encourage more senior citizens to travel. Opt 1) Talks about empty seats - not connected to fare reduction X Opt 2) lower fares attracting more Senior citizens - Supports the reason for reduction. ✓ Opt 3) No information that travel is discouraged. ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/docx/1774848677Rsng%20Paid%20-%2015%20copy_html_27093411.gif) opt 4) If they could already afford fares easily, reduction would not be necessary. X Hence, the implicit assumption is option 2.
